description:
You are a log-analyst who has just taken ownership of the toy project stored at
    /home/user/logtool.  
    Your goal is to re-configure the tool so that it starts detecting “sudo”
    attempts in the system logs and so that its thresholds are appropriate for the
    current workload.

    The repository already contains:
      • /home/user/logtool/config/settings.yaml  
      • /home/user/logtool/config/filters.toml  
      • /home/user/logtool/output/   ← this directory exists but is empty

    Work to do (5–10 terminal commands in total is a reasonable amount):
      1. Edit /home/user/logtool/config/settings.yaml  
         • Change alert_threshold from 90 to 75  
         • Change pattern_detection from false to true  
         • Append /var/log/kern.log as a third entry inside the existing log_paths
           list (leave the two original paths untouched and keep the order shown
           below).
      2. Edit /home/user/logtool/config/filters.toml  
         • Add a second [[rules]] table with the exact contents:
               name    = "flag_sudo_attempts"
               pattern = "sudo"
               action  = "alert"
      3. Create a new verification file
         /home/user/logtool/output/pattern_summary.log
         with exactly **three** lines, no trailing spaces:  
             SUMMARY_START  
             METRIC: sudo_attempts=FOUND  
             SUMMARY_END
         Capitalisation and punctuation must match exactly.
      4. Confirm that both configuration files are valid YAML / TOML
         (no syntax errors) and that the verification file exists with the
         precise line-by-line contents described above.

    Automated tests will verify:
      • The precise final text of /home/user/logtool/config/settings.yaml  
      • The precise final text of /home/user/logtool/config/filters.toml  
      • The presence and exact three-line content of
        /home/user/logtool/output/pattern_summary.log

    You do NOT need to actually run any log-parsing program; only the file
    edits and the summary log must be correct.  Feel free to display any console
    output that helps you check your work before finishing.
truth:
<!--
        Initial state (already present before the agent starts)
    -->
    <initialFiles>
        <file path="/home/user/logtool/config/settings.yaml"><![CDATA[
# LogTool configuration
alert_threshold: 90
pattern_detection: false
log_paths:
  - /var/log/syslog
  - /var/log/auth.log
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/logtool/config/filters.toml"><![CDATA[
# Default filter rules
[[rules]]
name = "ignore_health_checks"
pattern = "healthcheck"
action = "drop"
]]></file>
    </initialFiles>

    <!--
        Expected final state after the agent completes the task
    -->
    <finalFiles>
        <file path="/home/user/logtool/config/settings.yaml"><![CDATA[
# LogTool configuration
alert_threshold: 75
pattern_detection: true
log_paths:
  - /var/log/syslog
  - /var/log/auth.log
  - /var/log/kern.log
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/logtool/config/filters.toml"><![CDATA[
# Default filter rules
[[rules]]
name = "ignore_health_checks"
pattern = "healthcheck"
action = "drop"

[[rules]]
name = "flag_sudo_attempts"
pattern = "sudo"
action = "alert"
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/logtool/output/pattern_summary.log"><![CDATA[
SUMMARY_START
METRIC: sudo_attempts=FOUND
SUMMARY_END
]]></file>
    </finalFiles>
name: task_000000_d406fd59
